title: Podman fills tmpfs libpod events log
body:
### Issue Description

Podman is filling the /run/user/{uid}/libpod/tmp/events file when running healthchecks. Specifically the following logs are printed to the event log every check (per container).

{"ID":"1b40395248114496cf1e293e9fc08969b86749cf4fad7a1bcb9ee14677c78ed8","Image":"docker.io/redhat/ubi8:latest","Name":"event-log-full-1","Status":"health_status","Time":"2023-03-08T15:01:31.300060671-05:00","Type":"container","health_status":"healthy","Attributes":{"architecture":"x86_64","build-date":"2023-02-15T04:02:00","com.redhat.component":"ubi8-container","com.redhat.license_terms":"https://www.redhat.com/en/about/red-hat-end-user-license-agreements#UBI","description":"The Universal Base Image is designed and engineered to be the base layer for all of your containerized applications, middleware and utilities. {"ID":"1b40395248114496cf1e293e9fc08969b86749cf4fad7a1bcb9ee14677c78ed8","Image":"docker.io/redhat/ubi8:latest","Name":"event-log-full-1","Status":"exec_died","Time":"2023-03-08T15:01:31.328580501-05:00","Type":"container","Attributes":{"execID":"4607473ac4dbdb9f76a4262c0b543270bb1fa3871042f13352d1bc79cc208847"}}

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
```
podman network create event-log-full

podman pod create --name event-log-full --network event-log-full --share net
podman run \
         --name=event-log-full-1 -d \
         --pod=event-log-full \
         --health-cmd="echo hello" \
         docker.io/redhat/ubi8 tail -f /dev/null
```

Then monitor the event log
```
tail /run/user/100141/libpod/tmp/events/events.log -f
```

### Describe the results you received

Eventually the events file grows too large and podman stops being able to start/stop/interact with pods correctly due to the disk being full. For me, this is currently a 3GB file.

### Describe the results you expected

Continued, indefinite execution of a pod

comments.body:
Podman should rotate the event log file since v4.1, the size can be set in containers.conf.
Check `events_logfile_max_size`, it should default to 1 MB. Can you check your containers.conf files to see if this is set to 0 (unlimited) for some reason?

I apologize. I confirmed the content was being written to the events file using my dev machine, which is also where I acquired the actual podman info from. However, my production machine (which is the machine that actually filled up) looks to be using 4.0.2 instead of 4.2.0 and I didn't notice when I was comparing the two. I've updated the production machine's podman version and am closing the ticket.

Thank you
